function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

RESEARCH SUMMARY: MODERN ECOMMERCE TECH STACK FOR NEXT.JS-BASED ADULT STORE IN PARAGUAY

==========================================================================

1. CORE STACK: NEXT.JS + SUPABASE
==========================================================================

This is an excellent choice since the team already knows it from Vete. Here's the recommended architecture:

FRONTEND:
- Next.js 14+ with App Router (server components for SEO, client components for interactivity)
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Zustand or Jotai for client-side cart state (lightweight, no Redux overhead)
- next/image for optimized product image loading
- Server Actions for form handling (checkout, reviews, etc.)

BACKEND (Supabase):
- PostgreSQL database for products, orders, users, inventory
- Supabase Auth for user accounts (email/password, Google OAuth)
- Supabase Storage for product images (with image transformations)
- Supabase Edge Functions for server-side logic (payment webhooks, notifications)
- Row Level Security (RLS) for data protection
- Supabase Realtime for live inventory updates (optional)

KEY DATABASE TABLES:
- products (id, name, slug, description, price, sku, category_id, images, is_active)
- categories (id, name, slug, parent_id)
- product_variants (id, product_id, size, color, price_override, sku)
- inventory (id, product_variant_id, quantity, warehouse_location)
- orders (id, user_id, status, total, shipping_address, payment_method, payment_ref)
- order_items (id, order_id, product_variant_id, quantity, unit_price)
- users/profiles (id, email, name, phone, age_verified, addresses)
- cart (id, user_id, session_id for guest carts)
- cart_items (id, cart_id, product_variant_id, quantity)

WHY THIS WORKS FOR A SMALL TEAM:
- No separate backend to maintain
- Supabase handles auth, storage, DB, and realtime
- Free tier is generous (500MB DB, 1GB storage, 50K monthly active users)
- Pro plan at $25/month is very affordable when scaling

==========================================================================

2. PAYMENT INTEGRATION OPTIONS FOR PARAGUAY
==========================================================================

OPTION A: BANCARD vPOS (RECOMMENDED PRIMARY)
- This is THE standard for card payments in Paraguay
- Supports all local debit/credit cards (Visa, MasterCard, local cards)
- vPOS 2.0 provides a hosted payment page (redirect model)
- API integration available for custom checkout
- Integration flow:
  1. Create a "single buy" process via API
  2. Redirect user to Bancard's payment page or use their JS SDK
  3. Receive webhook confirmation
- Requirements: Must have a Paraguayan business (RUC) and bank merchant account
- Fees: ~3-4% per transaction (negotiable)
- Documentation: Available at comercios.bancard.com.py
- API endpoints: vpos.infonet.com.py (production), vpos.infonet.com.py:8888 (sandbox)
- NOTE FOR ADULT STORES: Bancard may have restrictions on adult content merchants. Verify with your bank/Bancard representative before committing.

OPTION B: MERCADOPAGO PARAGUAY
- Good alternative, well-known in the region
- Checkout Pro (redirect) or Checkout API (custom)
- Supports: credit/debit cards, MercadoPago wallet
- SDK available for Node.js
- Fees: ~4.49% + IVA per transaction in Paraguay
- Advantage: Users can pay with MercadoPago balance
- Integration: npm install mercadopago, use their SDK
- NOTE: MercadoPago has explicit policies AGAINST adult content merchants. This may be a dealbreaker. Review their terms carefully.

OPTION C: STRIPE VIA INTERMEDIARY
- Stripe doesn't operate directly in Paraguay
- Could use via a US/EU entity or intermediary service
- Options: Stripe Atlas ($500 to set up US company), or use a service like Payoneer
- More complex, higher fees, but supports international cards well
- Probably not worth it for a Paraguay-focused store

RECOMMENDED APPROACH:
- Primary: Bancard vPOS (covers local cards) - verify adult content policy first
- Secondary: Cash on delivery (contra entrega) - VERY common in Paraguay
- Tertiary: Bank transfer (transferencia bancaria) - manual but zero fees
- Consider: QR payments via Bancard (growing in Paraguay)

IMPORTANT: Adult content merchants face restrictions with most payment processors. Have a frank conversation with Bancard and your bank. If products are "wellness" or "intimate products" rather than explicit content, framing matters. Many stores in this category operate as "tienda de bienestar íntimo" rather than "sex shop."

==========================================================================

3. INVENTORY MANAGEMENT
==========================================================================

For a small team, build a simple custom system in Supabase:

APPROACH: Custom Admin Panel
- Build an admin dashboard in Next.js (protected routes)
- Track stock quantities per product variant
- Set low-stock alerts (Supabase trigger -> Edge Function -> WhatsApp notification)
- Simple stock movements log (additions, sales, adjustments, returns)

DATABASE DESIGN:
- inventory table tracks current quantity per variant
- inventory_movements table logs every change (type: sale, restock, adjustment, return)
- Use Supabase database triggers to auto-decrement on order placement
- Use database triggers for low-stock alerts

KEY FEATURES TO BUILD:
- Product CRUD with image upload to Supabase Storage
- Bulk import via CSV (for initial catalog load)
- Stock quantity editor with movement history
- Low stock report/dashboard
- Category management

WHY NOT USE A THIRD-PARTY:
- For a small adult store (likely <500 SKUs), a custom solution is simpler
- No monthly fees for inventory software
- Full control over the data model
- No risk of third-party platform rejecting adult products

==========================================================================

4. ORDER MANAGEMENT
==========================================================================

CUSTOM ORDER PIPELINE:
Status flow: pending_payment -> payment_confirmed -> preparing ->Both tools are returning auth errors. I'll provide my comprehensive research based on my extensive knowledge of these technologies, which is current through early 2025.

---

RESEARCH SUMMARY: Modern Ecommerce Tech Stack for Next.js-Based Adult Store in Paraguay

========================================================================
1. CORE STACK: NEXT.JS + SUPABASE
========================================================================

RECOMMENDATION: This is the right choice. The team already knows the stack from Vete, which drastically reduces development time.

Architecture:
- Next.js 14+ with App Router (server components for SEO, server actions for mutations)
- Supabase for: PostgreSQL database, Auth, Storage (product images), Edge Functions, Realtime (live inventory updates)
- Vercel for hosting (or self-hosted on a VPS if adult content policies are a concern -- see note below)

IMPORTANT: Vercel's Acceptable Use Policy may restrict adult content. Options:
  a) Self-host Next.js on a VPS (Hetzner, DigitalOcean, or local Paraguayan hosting like Tigo Business)
  b) Use Coolify or Dokku for easy self-hosting with Docker
  c) Check Vercel's policy carefully - "legal adult products" (like a sex shop) may be different from "adult content/pornography"

Supabase Database Schema (key tables):
- products (id, name, slug, description, price, category_id, images[], stock_count, sku, is_active, age_restricted, created_at)
- categories (id, name, slug, parent_id)
- orders (id, user_id, status, total, shipping_address, payment_method, payment_reference, created_at)
- order_items (id, order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price)
- customers (extends Supabase auth.users with phone, cedula_hash, age_verified, address)
- inventory_movements (id, product_id, quantity_change, reason, created_at)
- shipping_tracking (id, order_id, carrier, tracking_number, status, updated_at)

Supabase Row Level Security (RLS):
- Customers see only their own orders
- Admin users can CRUD everything
- Public can read active products only

========================================================================
2. PAYMENT INTEGRATION OPTIONS FOR PARAGUAY
========================================================================

Option A: BANCARD vPOS (RECOMMENDED as primary)
- The dominant card processor in Paraguay
- vPOS is their virtual POS for ecommerce
- Supports Visa, Mastercard, and local debit cards (Bancard Check)
- Integration: REST API, redirect-based or iframe checkout
- Fees: ~3-4% per transaction + monthly fee
- Requires: RUC (business tax ID), formal agreement with Bancard
- Settlement: In Guaranies (PYG) to local bank account
- Documentation: developers.bancard.com.py
- Flow: Create payment request via API -> redirect to Bancard checkout page -> webhook callback on completion

Option B: MERCADOPAGO PARAGUAY
- Available in Paraguay since ~2020
- Supports: Cards, MercadoPago wallet, bank transfers
- Checkout Pro (redirect) or Checkout API (custom form)
- Fees: ~4.5-5% + IVA per transaction
- Better developer experience than Bancard (better docs, SDKs)
- Settlement: To MercadoPago account, then withdraw to bank
- NOTE: MercadoPago may have restrictions on adult product categories. Check their TOS carefully.

Option C: STRIPE (via intermediary)
- Stripe does NOT operate directly in Paraguay
- Options: Use Stripe Atlas (form US LLC) or use a payment intermediary
- Complex, adds legal/tax overhead
- NOT recommended unless you need international cards specifically

Option D: CASH ON DELIVERY (COD)
- Critical for Paraguay where many people are unbanked or prefer cash
- Implement as a payment method in the order flow
- Driver collects payment and confirms in admin panel
- Very common in Paraguayan ecommerce (Pedidos Ya, etc.)

Option E: BANK TRANSFER
- Offer direct bank transfer with order reference number
- Customer uploads comprobante (receipt) via WhatsApp or upload form
- Admin confirms payment manually
- Very common in Paraguay

RECOMMENDED STRATEGY:
- Primary: Bancard vPOS (cards)
- Secondary: Bank transfer with manual confirmation
- Tertiary: Cash on delivery
- Optional: MercadoPago (if they allow the category)

========================================================================
3. INVENTORY MANAGEMENT
========================================================================

For a small team, build simple inventory directly in Supabase:

- Stock count on product table (simple decrement on order)
- inventory_movements table for audit trail (restocks, sales, adjustments, returns)
- Supabase Edge Function to check stock before order confirmation (prevent overselling)
- Use Supabase Realtime to update admin dashboard live
- Low-stock alerts: Supabase database webhook -> Edge Function -> WhatsApp notification to admin

Key features to build:
- Admin panel: Add stock, view movements, bulk import via CSV
- Automatic stock reservation on order creation (release after 30min if unpaid)
- Low stock threshold alerts (configurable per product)
- Simple barcode/SKU system for physical inventory counting

No need for external inventory management software for a small store. Build it into Supabase.

========================================================================
4. ORDER MANAGEMENT
========================================================================

Order lifecycle states:
  PENDING_PAYMENT -> PAYMENT_CONFIRMED -> PREPARING -> SHIPPED -> DELIVERED -> COMPLETED
  At any point: -> CANCELLED / REFUNDED

Build into admin dashboard:
- Order list with filters (status, date, customer)
- Order detail view with items, payment info, shipping info
- Status change buttons with automatic notifications (WhatsApp + email)
- Print packing slip / invoice
- Daily order summary report

Supabase implementation:
- orders table with status enum
- Database trigger on status change -> calls Edge Function -> sends notification
- RLS: customers see own orders, admins see all
- Use Supabase Realtime for live order notifications in admin panel

========================================================================
5. SHIPPING INTEGRATION
========================================================================

Paraguay shipping landscape:
- No major API-based shipping aggregators like in US/EU
- Common carriers: Correo Paraguayo (unreliable), private couriers

Practical approach for Paraguay:
a) SELF-DELIVERY in Asuncion/Gran Asuncion metro area
   - Most customers will be here
   - Use own delivery person or motorcycle courier service
   - Track via simple status updates in admin panel
   - Cost: Fixed rate by zone (Asuncion, Luque, San Lorenzo, Lambaré, etc.)

b) PRIVATE COURIER for interior cities
   - Companies like: La Encomendera, NSA Logistica, Flash Envios
   - Most don't have APIs - use manual tracking
   - Generate shipping label in admin, hand off to courier
   
c) PICKUP POINT
   - Allow customer to pick up at store/warehouse
   - Free, reduces shipping cost

Shipping rate calculation:
- Simple zone-based flat rates stored in Supabase
- Zone 1: Asuncion - Gs. 25,000
- Zone 2: Gran Asuncion - Gs. 35,000
- Zone 3: Interior - Gs. 50,000-80,000
- Free shipping threshold (e.g., orders over Gs. 500,000)

========================================================================
6. WHATSAPP BUSINESS API FOR NOTIFICATIONS
========================================================================

Options (from simplest to most robust):

Option A: WHATSAPP BUSINESS API via Meta Cloud API (RECOMMENDED)
- Free tier: 1,000 service conversations/month
- Requires Meta Business verification
- Send template messages for: order confirmation, shipping updates, delivery confirmation
- Implementation: HTTP API calls from Supabase Edge Functions
- Templates must be pre-approved by Meta

Option B: Third-party providers
- Twilio WhatsApp API - easier setup, better docs, but more expensive
- 360dialog - WhatsApp BSP, good for LATAM
- Gupshup - another option

Option C: Simple approach (MVP)
- Generate WhatsApp click-to-chat links (wa.me/595xxxxx?text=...)
- Admin manually sends updates
- Not scalable but works for launch

RECOMMENDED FLOW:
1. Customer places order -> Edge Function sends WhatsApp template "Order confirmed #{{order_id}}"
2. Payment confirmed -> WhatsApp template "Payment received for #{{order_id}}"
3. Order shipped -> WhatsApp template "Your order is on its way! Tracking: {{info}}"
4. Delivered -> WhatsApp template "Your order has been delivered. Rate us!"

Implementation:
- Store WhatsApp templates in Supabase
- Supabase Database Webhooks trigger on order status change
- Edge Function calls WhatsApp Cloud API with template + parameters
- Log all messages sent in a notifications table

PRIVACY NOTE for adult store:
- WhatsApp messages should be DISCREET
- Use generic business name (not "Sex Shop X")
- Template messages should not mention product details
- E.g., "Your order #1234 has been shipped" (not "Your vibrator has been shipped")

========================================================================
7. AGE VERIFICATION APPROACHES
========================================================================

Legal context: Paraguay requires buyers of adult products to be 18+. No specific digital age verification law like UK's (which was abandoned anyway).

Practical approaches (from simple to complex):

Level 1: AGE GATE (MINIMUM - implement this)
- Landing page with "Are you 18+?" confirmation
- Store consent in cookie/localStorage
- Not truly verification, but standard industry practice
- Use a tasteful, clear modal on first visit
- Set a cookie that expires in 30 days

Level 2: DATE OF BIRTH ENTRY
- Ask for date of birth instead of just yes/no
- Calculate age, reject if under 18
- Store DOB verification timestamp
- Slightly more friction = slightly more effective

Level 3: ACCOUNT-BASED VERIFICATION
- Require account creation to browse/purchase
- Collect cedula number (Paraguayan ID) at registration
- Self-declaration of age during signup
- Terms & conditions state 18+ requirement

Level 4: CEDULA VERIFICATION (if legally required)
- Validate cedula format and potentially cross-reference
- Paraguay doesn't have a public API for cedula validation easily accessible
- Could require cedula photo upload for manual verification
- Overkill for most cases

RECOMMENDED: Level 1 (age gate modal) + Level 3 (account required to purchase, with DOB and age confirmation in terms). This balances legal compliance with user experience.

Implementation:
- Middleware in Next.js checks for age_verified cookie
- If not set, redirect to age gate page
- Age gate sets cookie + stores in Supabase if user is logged in
- robots.txt and meta tags signal adult content appropriately

========================================================================
8. SEO FOR ADULT STORES
========================================================================

Challenges:
- Google Safe Search filters adult content
- Many ad platforms don't allow adult products
- Social media sharing is restricted

Technical SEO (Next.js advantages):
- Server-side rendering (SSR) and static generation (SSG) for product pages
- Proper meta tags: title, description, canonical URLs
- Structured data (JSON-LD) for products (price, availability, reviews)
- Clean URL structure: /categoria/vibradores/product-slug
- XML sitemap generated dynamically from Supabase products
- Fast Core Web Vitals (Next.js Image optimization, lazy loading)

Content strategy:
- Blog/educational content (sexual wellness, product guides)
- This content ranks better in search and drives organic traffic
- Use Next.js MDX or store blog posts in Supabase
- Target long-tail keywords in Spanish: "comprar vibrador en Paraguay", "sex shop Asuncion", "juguetes para adultos Paraguay"

Adult-specific SEO:
- Add RTA (Restricted to Adults) meta tag: <meta name="rating" content="adult">
- This helps content filters categorize the site correctly
- Register with adult-friendly search directories
- Don't try to hide the adult nature - be transparent

Local SEO:
- Google Business Profile (yes, sex shops can have one)
- Target "sex shop Asuncion", "sex shop Paraguay", "tienda adultos Paraguay"
- Local business schema markup
- Reviews/ratings on Google

Marketing channels (since ads are restricted):
- SEO/organic search (primary)
- WhatsApp marketing (with consent)
- Instagram (careful with content policies, focus on aesthetic/lifestyle)
- Twitter/X (more lenient with adult content)
- Telegram channel
- Email marketing
- Influencer partnerships
- Word of mouth / referral program

========================================================================
9. ANALYTICS
========================================================================

Option A: PLAUSIBLE ANALYTICS (RECOMMENDED)
- Privacy-focused, no cookies needed
- No GDPR/privacy banner required
- Simple, lightweight script
- Self-hostable or cloud ($9/mo)
- Perfect for a small team - shows what matters without overwhelm

Option B: GOOGLE ANALYTICS 4
- Free, powerful
- Requires cookie consent banner
- eCommerce tracking events: view_item, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, purchase
- May flag adult content sites (rare but possible)

Option C: POSTHOG (RECOMMENDED for product analytics)
- Open source, self-hostable
- Session recordings, feature flags, A/B testing
- Generous free tier (1M events/month)
- Great for understanding user behavior in the purchase funnel

Option D: UMAMI
- Open source, self-hostable
- Privacy-focused like Plausible
- Free if self-hosted

RECOMMENDED: Plausible (or Umami self-hosted) for traffic analytics + custom event tracking in Supabase for ecommerce metrics.

Custom ecommerce analytics to build in Supabase:
- Track in a page_views / events table
- Key metrics: conversion rate, average order value, top products, cart abandonment rate
- Build a simple admin dashboard with charts (use Recharts or Chart.js)
- Daily/weekly email reports via Edge Function + cron

========================================================================
COMPLETE RECOMMENDED TECH STACK SUMMARY
========================================================================

FRONTEND:
- Next.js 14+ (App Router, Server Components, Server Actions)
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui (already familiar from Vete)
- Zustand or Jotai for cart state management

BACKEND/DATABASE:
- Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime)
- Row Level Security for data isolation

HOSTING:
- Self-hosted on VPS (Hetzner/DigitalOcean) via Docker + Coolify
  OR Vercel if their AUP allows legal product sales
- Supabase Cloud (free tier to start, then Pro at $25/mo)

PAYMENTS:
- Bancard vPOS (primary - cards)
- Bank transfer (secondary)  
- Cash on delivery (tertiary)

NOTIFICATIONS:
- WhatsApp Cloud API (Meta) for order updates
- Email via Resend or Supabase's built-in email

ANALYTICS:
- Plausible or Umami (self-hosted) for traffic
- Custom Supabase tables for ecommerce metrics

SEARCH:
- Supabase full-text search (PostgreSQL tsvector)
- Or Meilisearch if catalog grows large

IMAGE HANDLING:
- Supabase Storage for product images
- Next.js Image component for optimization
- Consider Cloudflare Images or imgproxy for CDN + transforms

ADMIN PANEL:
- Custom-built with Next.js (same codebase, /admin routes)
- Protected by Supabase Auth with admin role check

========================================================================
ESTIMATED DEVELOPMENT TIMELINE (Small Team, 2-3 devs)
========================================================================

Phase 1 - MVP (4-6 weeks):
- Product catalog with categories
- Shopping cart
- Age gate
- Checkout with bank transfer + COD
- Basic admin panel (products, orders)
- WhatsApp click-to-chat (manual notifications)

Phase 2 - Payment + Notifications (2-3 weeks):
- Bancard vPOS integration
- WhatsApp Business API automated notifications
- Email notifications

Phase 3 - Polish (2-3 weeks):
- SEO optimization (sitemap, structured data, blog)
- Analytics setup
- Inventory management with alerts
- Shipping zone configuration
- Customer accounts with order history

Phase 4 - Growth (ongoing):
- Reviews/ratings system
- Wishlist
- Promotions/coupon system
- Referral program
- Advanced analytics dashboard

========================================================================
KEY RISKS & MITIGATIONS
========================================================================

1. Hosting restrictions: Self-host to avoid platform AUP issues
2. Payment processor restrictions: Bancard is local, less likely to restrict legal products. Verify with them upfront.
3. WhatsApp template rejection: Keep templates generic/discreet. Get pre-approved before launch.
4. Legal compliance: Consult Paraguayan lawyer on ecommerce + adult product regulations
5. Image storage: Supabase Storage is fine, but ensure images are served only to age-verified users (use signed URLs for explicit product images)

========================================================================

WHAT I DID:
- Attempted web searches and URL extractions for all 9 topic areas, but API authentication was unavailable
- Provided comprehensive research based on deep knowledge of all these technologies and the Paraguayan market context

WHAT I FOUND:
- Next.js + Supabase is an excellent fit - reuses team knowledge from Vete
- Bancard vPOS is the most practical primary payment method for Paraguay
- Cash on delivery and bank transfers are essential secondary methods
- WhatsApp Cloud API (Meta) is the best notification channel for Paraguay
- Simple age gate + account-based verification is sufficient
- Self-hosting is recommended to avoid platform content policy issues
- A practical MVP can be built in 4-6 weeks by a small team

FILES CREATED/MODIFIED: None (research-only task)

ISSUES ENCOUNTERED: Both mcp_web_search and mcp_web_extract returned 401 Unauthorized errors, so all research is based on existing knowledge rather than live web data. Core recommendations are still solid as these are well-established technologies and the Paraguayan payment/shipping landscape doesn't change frequently.
